source: Stern Admissions §Internal Transfer Applicants (line 121-123). GPA caveat per ARCHITECTURE.md §1604.
body:
**Internal transfer from CAS to Stern, verbatim from the Stern bulletin (Admissions §Internal Transfer Applicants):**

- **Application deadline:** *"by the March 1 application deadline"*. *"Stern accepts transfers in the fall only."*
- **Minimum credits:** *"Students must complete (or be in the process of completing) one full year (32 credits) of academic study in their original school of enrollment to be eligible for internal transfer."*
- **Disqualifier:** *"The Leonard N. Stern School of Business does not consider applicants who previously externally transferred to another school or college at NYU."*

**Required prerequisites by entry year:**
- **Sophomore entry:** 1 semester of calculus or higher; 1 semester of writing/composition (two semesters preferred).
- **Junior entry:** all of the sophomore requirements PLUS — 1 additional semester of calculus or higher; 1 semester of writing/composition; 1 semester of statistics; 1 semester of financial accounting; 1 semester of microeconomics.

**Important caveat: GPA thresholds are not published.** The Stern bulletin does not specify a numeric minimum GPA for internal transfer. Strong applicants typically present competitive grades, but no public floor exists. **Confirm any GPA expectations directly with Stern Admissions before applying.**

For course equivalencies (which CAS courses count for Stern prerequisites), see Stern's Course Equivalencies for Internal Transfers page.
